expectations.text: Proceeded because inputs (audience, goal, timeline) are rich
expectations.evidence: The response proceeds directly to the campaign brief with only a brief assumption note ('Assuming this is a SaaS time-tracking tool with a free-trial CTA; adjust any specifics if wrong'), without asking gating questions.
The Objective section states 'Drive 500 free-trial signups from freelance designers over 6 weeks' — a specific, numeric, time-bound goal.

expectations.text: Articulates the audience before->after belief shift
expectations.evidence: The Audience section explicitly states 'What they believe NOW: Tracking time is annoying admin overhead...' and 'What we want them to believe AFTER: Accurate time tracking takes 2 minutes a day...'

expectations.text: States a single key message plus a reason to believe
expectations.evidence: Single Key Message: 'You're probably billing for less than half the time you're actually working.' followed by a dedicated 'Reason to Believe' section with supporting proof points.

expectations.text: Includes CTA, channels, timeline, and success metrics
expectations.evidence: The brief includes a 'Call to Action' section ('Start your free trial — no credit card required'), a Channels table, a 6-week Timeline table, and a Success Metrics section with primary and secondary KPIs.
